A crimson dragon unleashes torrents of fire over granite battlements, melting iron gates as stone towers crack and collapse into smoking rubble beneath leathery wings blotting out the moon.
A bronze dragon scatters ten thousand soldiers across a muddy battlefield, its roar shaking siege engines apart as armored ranks dissolve into fleeing shadows beneath one thunderous wingbeat.
An obsidian dragon lands atop a jagged summit, folding continent-spanning wings as lightning splits the storm clouds behind its horned silhouette, talons cracking the ancient stone.
A magma-veined dragon erupts from a volcano's throat, ash and lava cascading off scales as old as the mountain itself, its emergence indistinguishable from the earth's fury.
A silver dragon rears over glittering treasure mounds, jaws snapping as armored knights encircle the hoard, their torches reflecting off a thousand coins and one furious, ancient eye.
Seven dragons arc across autumn skies—emerald, sapphire, amber, pearl—their synchronized flight casting racing shadows over golden forests and the kingdoms that watch in trembling awe.
A rider in black armor charges through collapsing shield walls astride a midnight-blue dragon, lance extended as gouts of frost freeze enemy cavalry mid-gallop across the bloodied plain.
A jade dragon sweeps low over a burning armada, igniting sails and masts with each breath as wooden warships explode into floating pyres across the blackened, smoke-choked sea.
A gold dragon slumbers atop a mountain of ancient treasure, each breath stirring coin-rivers and crown-piles in the torch-lit depths of a cavern older than written history.
Two titans—one ivory, one coal-black—tear at each other above snow-capped peaks, scales and blood raining onto glaciers as their aerial war shakes the valley below.
A white dragon circles above a stone-cathedral coronation, its shadow crossing the anointed king's face as bells toll and the crowd cannot tell if this is omen or benediction.

Archaeologists' torches illuminate a primordial dragon sealed in cathedral-sized ice, one amber eye visible through crystalline prison, ancient magic faltering as cracks spider outward through the frozen tomb.
Three obsidian eggs crack open in firelight, releasing squealing hatchlings with translucent wings and needle teeth, their mother's watchful golden eyes gleaming from the shadows beyond.
A rust-red dragon perches atop a conquered cathedral's spire, wings mantled wide over the occupied city below, declaration of dominion visible for thirty miles across the subject countryside.
A storm-grey dragon plummets through cloud layers, wings tucked tight as a stooping falcon, the mountain fortress below growing larger in seconds as defenders scramble and siege bells scream.

Lightning ignites the sky behind a violet dragon banking through thunderheads, electricity dancing between its horns and wingbones, each flash revealing scale-pattern and ancient scars in stark relief.
A bronze dragon marches beside ten thousand infantry across volcanic plains, its footfalls shaking the earth in rhythm with war drums, an empire's vengeance made flesh and scale.
An emerald dragon coils serpentine around a lighthouse tower three times over, neck arched possessively as fishing boats flee the harbor and its jeweled eyes track every fleeing sail.

A scarlet dragon lies broken in a scorched crater, dozens of ballista bolts piercing its wings, yet its head rises defiantly as approaching soldiers hesitate before the furnace-glow still burning in its throat.
A rose-gold dragon soars across a dawn sky painted crimson and amber, wings translucent against the rising sun, its cry echoing over misty valleys as the world awakens beneath its ancient shadow.
A pale blue dragon materializes from howling snow, frost coating its crystalline scales as it exhales blizzard-winds that freeze a charging warband into statues of ice and terror mid-scream.
A leviathan dragon erupts from storm-tossed ocean depths, seawater cascading off coral-encrusted scales as its wings unfold like tsunami waves, capsizing galleons in the maelstrom of its surfacing.
A cathedral-sized ribcage arcs over a desert caravan route, vertebrae forming a tunnel through sand dunes, ancient dragon bones bleached white and picked clean by centuries of wind.
A robed sorcerer stands before a coiled amethyst dragon in firelit ruins, staff raised as arcane symbols orbit between them—negotiation, confrontation, or communion frozen in one charged moment.

Forty dragons darken the autumn sky, flying in formation over mountain ranges toward unknown southern reaches, their migration an apocalypse and wonder witnessed once per generation by terrified shepherds below.
